note: Phase 4 Tracks B-G. Per the plan: pick by current pain. Promote a backlog item to a story file when you are about to start it.

items.title: Persistent tool-call logging
items.trigger: When stderr logs (Phase 2 default) stop being enough — you want to ask 'how much did I spend last week?' or 'which tool errored most yesterday?'
items.notes: Default shape: rotated ndjson files in a mounted volume. One line per tool call. Easy to grep, easy to ship to a real analytics store later if it ever matters. Reach for SQLite only if you need SQL-shaped questions; put it behind a small interface so the rest of the code does not care

items.title: /admin route in the PWA
items.trigger: Once persistent tool-call logging exists and the data is worth surfacing
items.notes: Surfaces: recent tool calls, spend over time, capture queue depth (if Phase 3's queue is in use), backup freshness card (P4 US-003), failed extractions. Auth: same as the rest of the PWA

items.title: Cost spike alerting
items.trigger: Always wanted; cheap; do once budget feels real
items.notes: If today's cost > 3x trailing 7-day average, send email via Resend/Mailgun (~free at this volume)

items.title: Weekly dedup audit script (scripts/audit_dedup.py)
items.trigger: When entity duplicates start showing up in answers
items.notes: Per entity type: pairwise similarity (Levenshtein/Jaro-Winkler/embedding cosine), flag pairs >0.85 into the observability store from Track B

items.title: /admin/dedup view for merging candidates
items.notes: Side-by-side cards; Merge into A/B or 'different' buttons; calls the merge_entities mutation from Phase 2 US-010

items.track: D - Entity types as throughput justifies
items.title: Add typed entities only when usage demands them
items.trigger: When you notice yourself wanting 'show me all things of type X'
items.notes: Don't pre-design taxonomy. Each typed entity is a few hours of work: define in Graphiti, update relevant tools to populate, optionally backfill

items.title: Add a turn-shaped vector layer alongside the graph
items.trigger: 3+ consecutive 'agent should have known X' incidents trace back to graph fragmentation (not missing episodes)
items.notes: Augments, not replaces, the graph; a vector store of full episode bodies, queried in parallel with Graphiti and unioned in results

items.title: iOS Shortcut polish
items.trigger: When the bare shortcut feels rough
items.notes: Async confirmation: shortcut returns immediately, follow-up notification on extraction complete ('captured 2 entities: Alice, Acme'); accept dictation in other languages

items.title: PWA polish
items.notes: Offline shell with read-only graph cache (last 100 query results); dark mode; search-as-you-type for past episodes

items.title: Long-form architecture writeup at app.{domain}/notes/architecture
items.trigger: Treat as the link in resume / cover letter / Twitter bio
items.notes: Sections: what this is, the bet (graph vs vector), the decisions, what surprised, what I'd do differently, what's next. No-auth public page served by the PWA. Source material lives in design-history.md

items.title: Add a second source migrator (Obsidian, Roam/Logseq, Apple Notes, plain-text journals, etc.)
items.trigger: When someone wants to seed the brain from a source other than Notion
items.notes: The Notion migrator is the first of many; others are demand-driven. Lift shared code into migrate/lib/ only if the duplication actually hurts. New source migrators live as sibling files under migrate/ (e.g. migrate/obsidian_to_graphiti.py) and reuse the existing graphiti_clients.py

items.title: Optional co-hosted Ollama for fully-local inference
items.trigger: Downstream user demand for 'data never leaves my box', or a contributor offers to land it
items.notes: Compose profile `--profile local-llm` spins up an ollama service on brainnet; Graphiti points at it via OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://ollama:11434/v1. The provider-neutral env surface already supports this for users with their own Ollama host; this story is the on-VPS variant + docs. Requires the user to size their VPS at 16GB+ RAM (vs the 8GB default); CPU-only extraction is ~30s/episode (vs <1s hosted) so audit the throughput acceptance with users before defaulting. Small models do worse on entity extraction than Haiku/Sonnet — call out the quality tradeoff in the README so users know what they're trading
